no_changes_rationale:
No code changes. This bead (ddx-723d93f2) is an epic/sub-epic whose AC is "All child beads closed." It is a tracker, not a unit of implementable work, and is far too large for a single execute-bead attempt (8 child beads spanning backend mutations, GraphQL resolver work, frontend consolidation, redirects, telemetry, docs).

Decomposition has already been done in a prior planning round; the child beads exist and carry the testable ACs:

- ddx-65543530 ‚Äî runs: canonicalize Run data projection (fix state_runs.go bundle layering; lossless AgentSession join) [8.0]
- ddx-6aedf547 ‚Äî runs: runRequeue mutation with mandatory idempotencyKey + atomic claims [8.1]
- ddx-22424dfc ‚Äî runs: merge Sessions/Executions detail into layer-aware Runs row expansion [8.2]
- ddx-d99a1dd1 ‚Äî runs: re-queue UI (try, run) + "Start worker from this drain" (work) [8.3]
- ddx-36d175ae ‚Äî runs: 302 redirects + internal link sweep + nav drop [8.4]
- ddx-4caba860 ‚Äî workers: project-scope audit + e2e [8.5]
- ddx-1978065c ‚Äî runs: reuse Story 6 search/chip primitives + Story 7 virtualization [8.6]
- ddx-d01e5017 ‚Äî runs: docs (FEAT-008/010/019/021) + telemetry audit events [8.7]

All 8 children currently status=open. Dependency edges already recorded (8.1‚Üí8.0, 8.2‚Üí8.0, 8.3‚Üí8.1+8.2, 8.4‚Üí8.2, 8.6‚Üí8.2 and Stories 6/7, 8.7‚Üí8.1+8.3).

Blocking: none ‚Äî this epic should remain in_progress until its children close. Closing the epic itself is the orchestrator's job once `ddx bead list --where parent=ddx-723d93f2` shows all children closed.

Follow-up: drain the children via `ddx work` in dependency order. 8.0 (smart tier) and 8.1 (smart tier) first; the rest are cheap-tier UI/wiring once the data + mutation foundation is in place.
